_meta.description: Aiko router test suite: 50 prompts covering chat, websearch, and agentic tool sequences

prompts.prompt: schedule a one-time reminder for tomorrow at 9am to submit the invoice
draft a calm message to my landlord about the broken heater and save it as a note
search for the latest llama.cpp release notes and summarize what changed
inspect think.py for the route_intent flow, find the agentic handoff, and summarize it
make a checklist for migrating the router examples, include risks, then save the note

debug why schedule_job custom weekdays fail, search the repo, read relevant files, plan a fix, and report

continue the memory consolidation refactor, inspect git context, search code paths, plan tests, and summarize remaining work
open agentic.py and think.py, map the full task-mode flow, inspect tool schemas, identify risks, plan tests, and write a report
set up an hourly scheduled job to check email from abc company and notify me
write a short README section explaining the route tracer and save it
look up current ROS2 Jazzy release notes and prepare a concise update summary
search the repository for _needs_websearch, read the implementation, and explain the route behavior
create a project plan for getting GRACE walking in 3 months with milestones and checkpoints
research current JetPack releases for Orin, fetch the source page, list evidence, and recommend whether to upgrade

resume the routing threshold tune-up, inspect router_prompts.json, run the route suite mentally, and list next edits
map all agentic tools, group them by workflow, identify missing validation, propose fixes, make a checklist, and save notes
remind me in 45 minutes to stretch
draft a professional deployment-delay update for my team and save it
research current RTX 5090 availability, compare pricing signals, write a short report, and save it
inspect util/test_route.py, find where suite prompts load, and explain how to add new cases
make a migration checklist for moving router data into JSON and include rollback steps
look up Modal's current pricing docs, fetch supporting pages, compare old assumptions, and answer
debug why the memory write loop swallows exceptions, search for handlers, read files, plan a fix, and summarize
choose between local embeddings on CPU or GPU for aarch64, research current constraints, compare, and recommend
continue the schedule refactor, list files, search call sites, inspect schedule.py, identify tests, and report remaining risks
audit the agentic loop for repeated tool calls, inspect policy code, map failure handling, plan regression tests, and write a summary
what do you think about combining architecture and coding as one route category
help me understand why binary routing is easier to test than many exclusive labels
is it weird that debugging router thresholds feels more satisfying than building features

talk me through whether planning should be a first step or just a tool choice
